title: 10 best herbal tinctures for loss of appetite
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for loss of appetite are concentrated plant extracts that stimulate appetite and aid in digestion. They work by enhancing digestive enzymes, regulating gut health, and providing essential nutrients. Some of the best herbal tinctures for loss of appetite include Ginkgo biloba, Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Panax ginseng, Astragalus membranaceus, Cinchona officinalis, Curcuma longa, Withania somnifera, Panax quinquefolius, and Piper nigrum. If left untreated, loss of appetite can lead to malnutrition, weight loss, fatigue, and decreased productivity, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with loss of appetite because they contain compounds that stimulate digestion and improve nutrient absorption, increasing the desire for food. The tannins present in the tincture also help to regulate blood sugar levels, reducing feelings of lethargy and fatigue that can accompany a lack of appetite. Additionally, the Maidenhair tree's adaptogenic properties allow it to balance the body's energy and promote overall well-being, encouraging individuals to engage in nourishing habits once again.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties help to protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to feelings of fatigue and lethargy that often accompany loss of appetite.

Memory-Enhancing Compounds: Ginkgo biloba contains compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids that have been shown to improve memory and cognitive function, which can help individuals with loss of appetite feel more motivated and interested in eating.

Vasodilatory Effects: The tincture's vasodilatory effects may also play a role in increasing appetite by improving blood flow to the digestive system, promoting healthy digestion, and reducing symptoms such as nausea and bloating that can accompany loss of appetite.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba, specifically quercetin and kaempferol, have antioxidant properties that may help to improve nutrient absorption and utilization, thus stimulating appetite.
Terpenoids (including bilobalide and ginkgolides): These compounds have neuroprotective effects that may help to alleviate stress and anxiety, which are often associated with loss of appetite.
Phenolic acids (including vanillic acid and caffeic acid): Phenolic acids in Ginkgo biloba may help to regulate gut health and improve nutrient absorption, which can contribute to increased appetite.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for tinctures due to their high concentration of flavonoids, which can stimulate appetite.
Seeds: Used for tinctures due to their galblan and bilobalide content, which can increase appetite and improve digestion.
Barks: Used for tinctures due to their flavonoid and terpene content, which can enhance appetite and digestive functions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees and clean them thoroughly to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the leaves in a warm dark place for 1-2 weeks or use a food dehydrator at 95°f for 8 hours.
Chop the dried leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife or a herb grinder to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped leaves with 80% vodka and 20% glycerin in a clean glass jar in a 1:2 leaf-to-solvent ratio.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks shaking the jar daily then strain and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense tinctures helps with loss of appetite because it has been traditionally used to stimulate digestion and improve gut health. The boswellic acids present in frankincense have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which may contribute to decreased appetite. Additionally, frankincense's ability to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety can also alleviate emotional eating and cravings, allowing individuals to develop a healthier relationship with food and restore their natural sense of hunger and fullness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as boswellic acids, may help alleviate digestive discomfort and inflammation associated with loss of appetite, allowing for improved nutrient absorption and overall appetite restoration.

Digestive stimulant: The bitter and pungent properties of frankincense tincture can stimulate digestion and improve gut motility, helping to increase appetite and reduce symptoms of indigestion or malabsorption.

Anxiolytic and antidepressant: Frankincense's ability to reduce anxiety and depression-like behaviors may help alleviate emotional eating disorders, which are often associated with loss of appetite, allowing for a more balanced relationship between food intake and overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Terpenoids (specifically Boswellic acids): These compounds help with loss of appetite by modulating inflammatory responses and reducing stress, which can be a contributing factor to decreased appetite.
Flavonoids (specifically Acuminatin): These compounds have been shown to improve gut health and enhance digestion, which can help stimulate appetite.
Alkaloids (specifically Triterpenoids): These compounds may help regulate gut hormones and improve appetite by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make tinctures due to their high concentration of boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help stimulate appetite.
Barks: They are used to make tinctures because of their ability to promote digestion and alleviate gastrointestinal issues, potentially increasing appetite.
Leaves: They are used to make tinctures due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate digestive issues and stimulate appetite.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of dried boswellia serrata resin, 2 cups of 80 proof vodka, and a clean glass jar with a lid.
Combine the dried resin with 2 cups of vodka in the glass jar, making sure the resin is completely submerged.
Seal the jar and place it in a cool dark location, allowing the mixture to steep for 2 to 3 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to smaller glass bottles, label them, and store them in a cool dark location.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with loss of appetite because it has a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other gastrointestinal issues that may be contributing to a lack of interest in food. The menthol in peppermint also has natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce nausea and discomfort associated with eating. This can make mealtime more enjoyable and encourage appetite to return. Additionally, peppermint's calming effects can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common underlying causes of loss of appetite.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Peppermint tincture helps stimulate digestion by increasing the movement of food through the digestive tract, which can be particularly beneficial for individuals who experience nausea or vomiting related to appetite loss.
Carminative Properties: The menthol content in peppermint tincture helps to relieve digestive discomfort, bloating, and cramps, reducing symptoms that may contribute to a lack of appetite.

Antispasmodic Effects: Peppermint tincture's antispasmodic properties can help relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and discomfort that may be associated with appetite loss.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol stimulates digestion and increases appetite by irritating the stomach and promoting the release of digestive enzymes.
Menthone: Menthone has a calming effect on the stomach and promotes appetite by reducing anxiety and stress-related digestive issues.
Rosmarinus acid (Rosmarinic acid): Rosmarinic acid has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to loss of appetite.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain the highest concentration of menthol, which helps stimulate appetite and improve digestion.
Roots: They are rich in sesquiterpene compounds that have been traditionally used to enhance appetite and relieve digestive issues.
Stems: They contain a moderate amount of menthol and other volatile oils that help to increase appetite and alleviate digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers on a sunny day.
Chop 1 part of the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped plant material with 2 parts of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 4 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with loss of appetite because it stimulates digestion and increases gut motility, which can help to alleviate digestive discomfort that may be contributing to a lack of appetite. Additionally, ginger's natural anti-inflammatory properties may reduce nausea and vomiting, making food more appealing again. The warming sensation it produces in the body can also increase blood flow to the stomach, encouraging appetite and promoting healthy digestion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal ginger tinctures contain compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce nausea and discomfort associated with loss of appetite.

Digestive stimulant properties: Ginger has natural digestive stimulants that can increase saliva production, improve digestion, and relieve symptoms of indigestion and bloating, making food more palatable and increasing appetite.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: Herbal ginger tinctures contain antioxidants and antimicrobials that can help reduce oxidative stress, inflammation, and infections in the gut, which can contribute to loss of appetite.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, particularly 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, have been shown to stimulate appetite and improve digestion by increasing gastric motility and secretions, which can help alleviate loss of appetite.

Shogaols: Shogaols, such as 6-shogaol and 8-shogaol, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with loss of appetite.

Curcumenol: Curcumenol, a sesquiterpene present in ginger, has been found to have appetite-stimulating properties, possibly through its effects on gastric acid secretion and motility, which can help improve appetite and digestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves: Contain some medicinal properties, although they are not as potent as the rhyzome or root.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh rhizomes of zingiber officinale for tincture production.
Clean and slice the rhizomes into small pieces weighing 1-2 pounds for maceration.
Combine the sliced rhizomes with 80-100 proof ethanol at a 1:2 ratio by weight for extraction.
Steep the mixture in a dark glass container for 2-6 weeks to allow for extraction.
Strain the liquid from the solids using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with loss of appetite because it contains curcumin, a bioactive compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin stimulates digestion and absorption of nutrients, which can help improve appetite and reduce feelings of fullness. Additionally, turmeric's warming nature can increase blood flow to the digestive tract, stimulating hunger hormones and promoting healthy gut function. By addressing underlying digestive issues and improving nutrient absorption, turmeric tinctures can help restore a healthy appetite and support overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids, a group of compounds found in turmeric, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, leading to improved appetite.

Bioactive Compounds: Turmeric tincture contains bioactive compounds such as gingerols, shogaols, and paradols, which have been shown to stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and nausea, making it easier to maintain a healthy appetite.

Cytokine Modulation: Turmeric's active compound curcumin has been found to modulate cytokines, which are small proteins that regulate inflammation and immune responses. This modulation can help to reduce the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines that may contribute to loss of appetite.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin helps with loss of appetite by regulating appetite hormones and reducing inflammation in the digestive system, which can improve nutrient absorption and utilization.
Tumerone: Tumerone helps with loss of appetite by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can improve digestion and nutrient absorption, thereby increasing appetite.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC helps with loss of appetite by inhibiting the activity of certain enzymes involved in the breakdown of nutrients, thereby increasing the availability of nutrients and improving appetite.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part for tinctures due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as curcumin, which is known for its appetite-stimulating properties.
Roots: Roots of Curcuma longa are also used to make tinctures, as they contain curcumin and other beneficial compounds that can help stimulate appetite.
Seeds: Although not as widely used, seeds of Curcuma longa may be used to make tinctures, possibly due to their lesser-known but still potentially beneficial effects on appetite and digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh turmeric roots in the early morning when they are at their peak potency.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces and dry them in a single layer at 100-150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine the dried root pieces with 80 proof vodka and a 1:2 ratio of root to solvent in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar every day to ensure even extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discard the solids and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with loss of appetite because they stimulate digestion and increase saliva production, which can help to break down food more efficiently. The bitter compounds in dandelion root also stimulate the appetite by increasing the release of digestive enzymes, making it easier for the body to process nutrients. Additionally, dandelion tinctures have anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe digestive issues that could be contributing to a lack of appetite. As a result, taking dandelion tinctures can help to restore a healthy relationship with food and promote a sense of hunger and satisfaction.
remedy_properties: Laxative Properties: Dandelion tincture helps stimulate digestion and relieve constipation, which can contribute to a decrease in appetite by increasing gut motility and improving nutrient absorption.
Bitters: The bitter compounds found in dandelion tincture stimulate the appetite by activating digestive enzymes and increasing saliva production, making food more enjoyable and easier to digest.

Stomach Soothing Properties: Dandelion tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an irritated stomach, reducing discomfort and pain associated with eating, which can often lead to a decrease in appetite.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol, a triterpenoid saponin, has been shown to have a stimulating effect on the appetite by increasing the secretion of digestive enzymes and enhancing gut motility.

Taraxasterol acetate: The acetate form of taraxasterol has been found to have a similar appetite-stimulating effect as taraxasterol, possibly due to its ability to regulate gut hormone secretion and enhance nutrient absorption.

Taraxanthone: Taraxanthone, a phenolic compound, has been reported to have a mild appetite-stimulating effect, possibly due to its ability to regulate gut motility and enhance the absorption of nutrients.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used because they contain inulin, a type of fiber that can help stimulate appetite.
Leaves: They are used because they contain bitter compounds like sesquiterpene lactones, which can help increase appetite by stimulating digestion.
Flowers: They are used because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help improve digestion and increase appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-50 fresh taraxacum officinale roots in the fall or spring when they are most potent for tincture making.
Chop the taraxacum officinale roots into small pieces to release their active compounds and increase surface area for extraction.
Combine 250g of chopped taraxacum officinale root with 750ml of high-proof ethanol in a clean glass container for maceration.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2-4 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container to collect the clear tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with loss of appetite because it stimulates the digestive system, increasing saliva production and gut motility. This helps to break down food more efficiently, making nutrients more accessible and alleviating discomfort or pain associated with eating. Additionally, licorice root contains glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that may soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, allowing individuals to feel more comfortable and inclined to eat. As a result, herbal licorice tinctures can help restore appetite and improve overall digestion.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation, making it easier to feel like eating again.

Flavonoids: Licorice tincture contains flavonoids, such as glabridin and isoliquiritigenin, which have been shown to stimulate appetite and improve digestion by increasing gastric motility and reducing nausea.

Isomaltose: This natural sweetener found in licorice root has been shown to increase appetite and improve food intake by activating the brain's reward centers and releasing feel-good hormones like dopamine.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory properties and stimulates appetite by reducing stress and promoting the release of gut hormones involved in appetite regulation.

Flavonoids (specifically, isoorientin and isovitreoside): These polyphenolic compounds exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help to regulate gut motility and alleviate digestive discomfort, ultimately increasing appetite.

Licopyrone: This phenolic compound has been shown to have adaptogenic properties, which can help to reduce stress and promote appetite by regulating the body's response to stress and promoting the release of appetite-stimulating hormones.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhiza glabra roots are the most commonly used part to make tinctures for loss of appetite due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that stimulates appetite.
Leaves: Glycyrrhiza glabra leaves are used to make tinctures for loss of appetite due to their ability to enhance the overall digestive system and stimulate appetite.
Seeds: Glycyrrhiza glabra seeds are used to make tinctures for loss of appetite due to their rich content of flavonoids and saponins that aid in digestion and stimulate appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra from a reputable source when fully matured.
Chop the roots into small pieces with a sharp knife to increase their surface area.
Combine the chopped roots with 80 proof vodka or ethanol in a 1:5 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a glass container with a lid for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids to obtain the final tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm tinctures helps with loss of appetite because they contain mucilages that soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the digestive tract. This calming effect can help reduce inflammation and irritation, allowing the body to focus on nutrient absorption rather than fighting off discomfort. As a result, individuals may find their sense of hunger returns, and they are able to enjoy meals without the burden of chronic discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Slippery elm tincture reduces inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), Crohn's disease, and ulcerative colitis, all of which can cause loss of appetite.

Mucilaginous properties: The mucilage present in slippery elm soothes and protects the mucous membranes lining the digestive tract, reducing irritation and inflammation that can lead to decreased appetite.

Gastroprotective effects: Slippery elm tincture has been shown to reduce gastric ulcers and protect against stomach acid damage, which can help alleviate symptoms of nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain associated with loss of appetite.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: A flavonoid that acts as an antioxidant, helping to reduce inflammation in the body and stimulate appetite by promoting the production of appetite-stimulating hormones.
Salicin: A phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate underlying conditions contributing to loss of appetite, such as pain or digestive issues.
Ellagic acid: A polyphenol that may help stimulate appetite by improving gut health, reducing inflammation, and promoting the release of appetite-stimulating hormones in the gut.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ulmus rubra are used to make tinctures because they contain compounds that may have a stimulating effect on the appetite and digestive system.
Roots: Ulmus rubra roots are used to make tinctures due to their potential adaptogenic and stimulatory properties, which may help increase appetite and improve overall well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh ulmus rubra leaves and twigs from a pesticide-free area on a dry morning.
Clean the harvested material by gently brushing off dirt and debris with a soft-bristled brush.
Chop the cleaned ulmus rubra into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped ulmus rubra with 500ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with loss of appetite because it stimulates digestion and improves gut health. The herb's natural properties increase saliva production, breaking down food more efficiently, and reducing bloating and discomfort that can lead to a decrease in appetite. Additionally, fennel tincture has been shown to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, easing cramps and discomfort associated with eating, allowing individuals to regain their appetite and enjoy meals without hesitation.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Fennel tincture has carminative properties, which means it helps to relieve flatulence and bloating, making it easier for people with a suppressed appetite to feel comfortable eating again.
Digestive stimulant property: Fennel tincture also stimulates digestion, which can help to improve nutrient absorption and reduce nausea or discomfort that may be contributing to loss of appetite.

Gastrokinetic property: The gastrokinetic properties of fennel tincture help to increase the movement of food through the digestive system, reducing feelings of fullness and bloating, making it easier for people with a suppressed appetite to eat again.
remedy_constituents: Fenchone: This terpene has carminative and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce nausea and discomfort, thereby encouraging appetite.
Anethole: This phenolic compound has a sweet, anise-like flavor and aroma, which can stimulate the appetite and reduce symptoms of nausea and indigestion.

Umbellulone: Although less commonly discussed than other compounds, some research suggests umbellulone, another terpene found in Foeniculum vulgare, may have appetite-stimulating effects due to its ability to reduce gastrointestinal inflammation and improve digestion.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their carminative properties, which help to reduce gas and alleviate digestive discomfort.
Leaves: They are used because of their high content of volatile oils, particularly anethole, which has a stimulatory effect on appetite.
Roots: They are used for their rich content of inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps to regulate digestive health and stimulate appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh or dried foeniculum vulgare roots and stems in the morning to ensure optimal potency.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase the surface area.
Combine 100 grams of chopped foeniculum vulgare with 500 milliliters of 40% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 to 21 days to allow for extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with loss of appetite because its calming and soothing properties can ease digestive discomforts, such as bloating, gas, and indigestion, which are common triggers for decreased appetite. The tincture's natural sedative effects can also help reduce anxiety and stress that may be contributing to a lack of interest in food. Additionally, lemon balm's mild antidepressant properties may help improve overall mood, making it more enjoyable to eat and increasing the desire for nourishment.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's high antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to a decrease in appetite by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Anxiolytic properties: The flavonoids present in lemon balm have been shown to possess anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of depression and anxiety that often accompany loss of appetite.

Digestive stimulant properties: Lemon balm has natural carminative and digestive stimulating properties, which can help improve digestion, reduce nausea and bloating, and increase appetite by promoting a healthy gut microbiome.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic Acid: A phenolic compound that helps to stimulate appetite by acting as a natural antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that may be contributing to loss of appetite.

Volatile Oils (Linalool and Linalyl Acetate): A group of terpenes that have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that may be causing a loss of appetite, and also stimulate appetite by promoting relaxation.
Volatile Oil (Menthone): A terpene that has a stimulating effect on the digestive system, which can help to increase appetite by improving digestion and reducing nausea.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly citral and citronellal, which have appetite-stimulating properties.
Flowers: They are used as a substitute for leaves and contain similar properties, making them a popular choice for herbal remedies aimed at improving appetite.

Stems: Although less commonly used than leaves and flowers, stems of Melissa officinalis may be used in some herbal preparations to support appetite regulation, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa leaves in the early morning or late afternoon when the plant is at its highest potency level.
Dry the leaves in a single layer on paper bags or a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 50 grams of dried melissa leaves with 500 milliliters of 80 percent ethanol in a glass container and store in a cool dark place for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container discarding the solids and reserve the liquid.
Transfer the liquid to a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid and store in the refrigerator to slow down the aging process for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with loss of appetite because its soothing properties can calm an upset stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort that may be contributing to a lack of appetite. Additionally, chamomile's gentle sedative effects can help alleviate anxiety and stress-related digestive issues, allowing the body to relax and focus on nutrient absorption instead of feeling overwhelmed by symptoms. As a result, herbal chamomile tinctures can help stimulate a healthy appetite and promote overall digestive well-being.
remedy_properties: Calming effects: Chamomile's calming properties can help reduce anxiety and stress, which are common underlying causes of decreased appetite, making it easier to eat and increasing overall interest in food.

Digestive soothing: Chamomile has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help soothe digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), stomach upset, and acid reflux, which can contribute to a lack of appetite.

Anxiolytic and sedative properties: The anxiolytic and sedative effects of chamomile tincture can promote relaxation and reduce irritability, making it easier for individuals with depression or other mood disorders to feel like eating again.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that has been shown to stimulate appetite and improve digestion.
Flowers: Contain bisabolol, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe the digestive system and increase appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning when dewy for optimal potency and quality.
Cleanly chop 1 cup of harvested matricaria chamomilla flowers into smaller pieces to facilitate infusion.
Combine chopped matricaria chamomilla with 2 cups of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring the plant material is completely submerged.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate extraction and maceration.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and transferring the tincture to amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng tinctures helps with loss of appetite because it stimulates digestion and increases blood flow to the digestive system, making food more appealing. The herbal extract also boosts energy levels and mental clarity, reducing fatigue and stress that can lead to a lack of appetite. Additionally, ginseng's adaptogenic properties help regulate metabolism, promoting healthy weight management and appetite balance. As a result, ginseng tinctures can effectively alleviate the symptoms of loss of appetite, allowing individuals to regain their normal eating habits.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ginseng helps to adapt the body's response to stress, which can contribute to loss of appetite, by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
Stimulating digestion: Ginseng has been shown to stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut motility, helping to increase appetite and improve nutrient absorption.
Antioxidant properties: Ginseng contains antioxidants that help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to decreased appetite and overall poor health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the ginseng plant contain bioactive compounds that help stimulate appetite and improve digestion, making them effective for treating loss of appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh panax ginseng roots and rinse them under running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and mix them with vodka in a glass jar at a ratio of 1 part herb to 5 parts solvent.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass jar discarding the solids and squeezing the cloth gently.
Transfer the filtered tincture to dark amber glass bottles and store them in the refrigerator at 40°f for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tinctures helps with loss of appetite because it stimulates the digestive system, promoting a healthy gut microbiome. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in yarrow tincture have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with decreased appetite. Additionally, yarrow's bitter compounds stimulate digestion and increase salivation, making food more palatable and enjoyable, ultimately helping to restore appetite and improve overall nutrient absorption. This natural approach can be especially beneficial for individuals experiencing chronic illness or undergoing cancer treatment.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Yarrow's antispasmodic properties can help relax digestive muscles, reducing cramping and discomfort associated with poor appetite, allowing the body to better absorb nutrients.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is often linked to loss of appetite, and promote a healthy gut environment that supports nutrient absorption.
Stimulant properties: Yarrow's stimulant properties can help increase blood flow to the digestive system, stimulating digestion and improving appetite by increasing the desire for food.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are effective in stimulating appetite and alleviating digestive issues.
Seeds: Containing essential oils and nutrients, seeds help regulate digestion and improve appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh yarrow flowers and leaves in the early morning or late afternoon for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation or desiccation methods within 24 hours to preserve bioactive compounds.
Grind 50 grams of dried achillea millefolium into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for efficient extraction.
Combine the powdered herb with 100 proof vodka or isopropyl alcohol in a 1:2 ratio and steep for 14 days to allow solubilization of bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into dark glass bottles, discarding solids, and store tincture at room temperature for up to 24 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with loss of appetite because its active compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, have been shown to stimulate digestion and increase appetite in individuals with suppressed hunger. The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties may also help alleviate digestive discomfort, reducing nausea and stomach pain that can contribute to a decrease in food intake. Additionally, the herb has been traditionally used to soothe upset stomachs, which can lead to improved nutrient absorption and a sense of fullness and satisfaction.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to a lack of appetite.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of pot marigold tincture may help protect against oxidative stress and damage to gut lining, leading to improved digestion and increased appetite.

Digestive stimulant properties: Pot marigold has been traditionally used as a digestive stimulant, which can help increase appetite by improving the body's ability to digest food and absorb nutrients.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of the pot marigold plant have been traditionally used to stimulate appetite and improve digestion.
Flowers: The bright yellow flowers of the pot marigold plant contain carotenoids and flavonoids that can help to increase appetite and reduce nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality calendula flowers from your garden or local nursery for maximum potency.
Dry the fresh calendula flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Combine one part dried calendula flowers with one part high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar for infusion.
Allow the mixture to steep for 6 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily for maximum extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and label it correctly for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover tinctures helps with loss of appetite because it stimulates the digestive system, increasing hunger and improving nutrient absorption. The tannins present in red clover have been shown to enhance gastric motility, which enables the stomach to move food along efficiently and effectively. Additionally, red clover's flavonoids work synergistically to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, allowing for optimal digestion and absorption of nutrients. This results in a sense of satisfaction and renewed appetite, making it an effective natural remedy for individuals struggling with loss of appetite.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover contains isoflavones, which have been shown to stimulate appetite and improve digestion by increasing the production of gastric acid and improving gut motility.

Phytoestrogens: The phytoestrogens present in red clover tincture can help regulate hormone imbalances that may contribute to loss of appetite, particularly in menopausal women or individuals with hormonal deficiencies.

Anthocyanins: Red clover's anthocyanins have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract, which can contribute to reduced appetite.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of red clover contain a high concentration of flavonoids and saponins, which can help stimulate appetite and improve digestive health.
Leaves: The leaves of red clover are rich in vitamins and minerals that support overall health and well-being, making them an effective addition to medicinal tinctures for loss of appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part fresh or dried trifolium pratense flowers at peak blooming stage for optimal potency.
Clean and chop the harvested material into small pieces to facilitate proper extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 25% volume vodka or neutral grain spirit in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily, allowing for optimal solvent extraction.
Strain and filter the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the tincture from solids before transferring to amber glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with loss of appetite because it has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress that can contribute to a decrease in appetite. By promoting relaxation and easing digestion, valerian tinctures can increase hunger and improve food cravings, making it easier to eat and maintain a healthy diet. Additionally, valerian's sedative properties can help alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders, such as irritable bowel syndrome, which may also be related to loss of appetite.
remedy_properties: Sedative and Anti-Anxiety Properties: Valerian root has been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress-related appetite suppression.
Digestive Soothing Effects: Valerian root contains compounds that can soothe the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort that may contribute to loss of appetite.

GABA-Receptor Modulation: Valerian root has been shown to modulate GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) receptors in the brain, which can help regulate appetite and satiety hormones, leading to increased food intake.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in valerenic acid, a key component responsible for the plant's sedative and anti-anxiety effects, making them effective in stimulating appetite.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, contain a high concentration of alkaloids that have been shown to increase appetite and reduce nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality valeriana officinalis roots and dry them properly for at least two weeks to preserve potency.
Chop the dried valeriana officinalis roots into small pieces and combine them with 60% vodka in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the tincture.
Store the valeriana officinalis tincture in an amber-colored bottle, diluting it with water as needed for a dosage of 10-20 drops three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn tinctures helps with loss of appetite because its unique combination of flavonoids, procyanidins, and other bioactive compounds can stimulate the appetite center in the brain. The herb's ability to improve circulation and relax smooth muscle tissues can also help reduce digestive discomfort, making it easier for the body to absorb nutrients and process food. Additionally, hawthorn's adaptogenic properties allow it to balance the body's energy levels, reducing fatigue and increasing motivation to eat.
remedy_properties: Cardiotonic and Vasodilatory Effects: Hawthorn's cardiotonic and vasodilatory effects can help to improve blood flow and circulation, which can increase hunger and appetite by delivering oxygen and nutrients to the digestive system.

Anxiolytic and Sedative Properties: Hawthorn's anxiolytic and sedative properties can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common underlying causes of loss of appetite, allowing individuals to feel more relaxed and inclined to eat.

Stomach Soothing and Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Hawthorn's stomach-soothing and anti-inflammatory effects can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, which can cause discomfort and pain that may contribute to a decrease in appetite.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, Hawthorn roots have been traditionally used to stimulate appetite and improve digestion.
Fruits: Hawthorn fruits are rich in flavonoids and anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help increase appetite and promote a healthy gut.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried crataegus monogyna leaves and stems.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and mix with 70% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding solids.
Label the tincture and store it in a cool dark place for up to two years, using one half teaspoon twice daily as needed.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to loss of appetite that are treated with herbal tinctures are nausea, indigestion, and digestive issues. Tinctures like Ginger (Zingiber officinale) and Peppermint (Mentha piperita) can help alleviate symptoms of nausea and indigestion by soothing the stomach and reducing inflammation. Other herbs like Chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla) and Fennel (Foeniculum vulgare) may also be used to treat digestive issues such as bloating and gas.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with loss of appetite is a blend of Ashwagandha, Ginger, and Licorice root. Ashwagandha helps to balance the body's energy and reduce stress, which can contribute to a lack of appetite. Ginger stimulates digestion and increases appetite, while Licorice root soothes the stomach and alleviates nausea. Taking these tinctures together can help to restore a healthy appetite and promote overall digestive well-being. Consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures helps with loss of appetite because it improves blood flow to the brain, enhancing cognitive function and memory. This increase in mental clarity can stimulate appetite and interest in food. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress, promoting overall health and well-being. By addressing the underlying causes of decreased appetite, such as fatigue and mental fogginess, Ginkgo biloba tinctures can support individuals in regaining their appetite and enjoying a balanced diet. This can be particularly beneficial for those experiencing appetite loss due to stress or illness.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help alleviate loss of appetite by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to decreased appetite.

Terpenoids (specifically Bilobalide): Terpenoids, such as Bilobalide, aid in improving appetite by acting as a neuroprotectant and reducing the damage caused by free radicals in the brain, which can lead to decreased appetite.
Ginkgolides: These compounds help increase appetite by modulating the activity of certain neurotransmitters, such as serotonin and dopamine, which play a crucial role in appetite regulation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used to make tinctures for loss of appetite due to their rich flavonoid and terpenoid content, which is believed to stimulate appetite.
Seeds: The seeds are used to make tinctures for loss of appetite as they contain ginkgolic acid, which may help increase hunger and improve appetite.
Buds: The buds are used to make tinctures for loss of appetite, possibly due to their ability to stimulate digestive processes and increase appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in large quantities during the fall season when they are most potent.
Clean the ginkgo biloba leaves thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris that may be present.
Combine 250 grams of the cleaned ginkgo biloba leaves with 750 milliliters of a high-proof ethanol or glycerin in a large container.
Steep the mixture for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place to allow the tincture to develop its potency.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the liquid tincture from the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with loss of appetite because they contain compounds that stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut health. The active components, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce nausea and inflammation in the digestive tract. This can help to increase appetite and improve food digestion. Additionally, the warming and anti-inflammatory properties of Zingiber officinale can also help to stimulate the digestive system and promote a sense of well-being, making it easier to eat and feel hungry again.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut motility, increasing appetite and reducing nausea.
Shogaols: Have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of loss of appetite.
Terpenes (specifically, sesquiterpenes): Act as a digestive stimulant, promoting the release of digestive enzymes and improving nutrient absorption, which can help increase appetite.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used to make tinctures due to their high concentration of active compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which stimulate appetite.
Roots: Roots are used to make tinctures because they contain a higher concentration of bioactive compounds compared to other parts, making them effective in treating loss of appetite.
Buds: Buds are used to make tinctures as they are rich in volatile oils, which can help stimulate appetite and improve digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh zingiber officinale roots in early morning with a sharp knife.
Cut the roots into small pieces and dry them in a warm dry place for 1-2 weeks.
Combine 1 part dried zingiber officinale roots with 2 parts 80-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking and stirring.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with loss of appetite because they contain compounds such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties may help to soothe the digestive system and reduce stress, two common causes of loss of appetite. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has been shown to stimulate the appetite and improve nutrient absorption in the gut, making it a potential natural remedy for individuals experiencing a decrease in appetite. It may also help to alleviate underlying digestive issues contributing to the loss of appetite.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin helps stimulate appetite by increasing the production of stomach acid and improving digestion.
Licoricidin: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nausea and discomfort associated with loss of appetite.
Licorinic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant properties that can help protect the stomach lining and improve digestion, leading to increased appetite.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are commonly used due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that stimulates appetite and improves digestion.
Leaves: The leaves are used to create tinctures that help to increase appetite, as they contain smaller amounts of glycyrrhizin but still contribute to digestive health.
Root Buds: The root buds are also used for their medicinal properties, including appetite stimulation, due to their unique combination of glycosides and other bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the early morning after the plant has been dormant for a year.
Dry the roots in a warm place with good air circulation for 2 weeks at 35 degrees celsius.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part of the powdered root with 2 parts of 70% ethanol in a glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place then strain the liquid and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng tinctures helps with loss of appetite because of its adaptogenic properties, which help the body adapt to stress and promote a sense of balance. The tincture's active compounds, such as ginsenosides, stimulate digestion and improve nutrient absorption, leading to increased appetite. Additionally, Panax ginseng tinctures have been traditionally used to enhance energy and vitality, which can also contribute to a healthier appetite. This can be particularly beneficial for individuals experiencing fatigue or stress-related appetite loss.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These compounds help stimulate appetite and improve digestion by enhancing the absorption of nutrients and increasing the production of digestive enzymes.
Saponins: Saponins have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce nausea and vomiting associated with loss of appetite.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help regulate appetite and improve digestion by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain the highest concentration of ginsenosides, which are believed to stimulate appetite and improve digestion.
Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that may help increase appetite and improve overall gut health.
Barks: They contain bioactive compounds that may help stimulate appetite and improve digestion by regulating gut hormones.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried panax ginseng roots in late summer or early fall to ensure optimal potency.
Chop the roots into small pieces and combine them with 500ml of 95% ethanol in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container discarding the solids.
Filter the tincture through a 0.2 micron filter and store it in dark glass bottles at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus tinctures helps with loss of appetite because they are believed to stimulate the digestive system and increase the production of digestive enzymes. This helps to improve nutrient absorption and utilization, which can help to increase appetite and reduce feelings of nausea and fatigue. The tincture's adaptogenic properties also help to balance the body's energy and reduce stress, which can contribute to loss of appetite. As a result, Astragalus membranaceus tinctures may help to alleviate loss of appetite and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds help stimulate appetite by increasing the secretion of gastric juices and improving gut motility.
Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates help boost appetite by activating the body's immune system and reducing inflammation, which can contribute to loss of appetite.

Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds have antioxidant properties that help protect against oxidative stress, which can lead to loss of appetite, and also help regulate appetite by modulating the gut-brain axis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Astragalus membranaceus are commonly used because they contain the highest concentration of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and saponins, which may help stimulate appetite.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Astragalus membranaceus are used due to their rich content of astragalosides, which may help increase appetite by regulating digestive system function.

Stems: The stems of Astragalus membranaceus are used, although less commonly than roots or rhyzomes, due to their secondary compounds that may also contribute to increased appetite by enhancing digestive processes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature astragalus membranaceus roots with a sharp knife and clean them thoroughly to remove dirt.
Chop the roots into small pieces with a sharp knife and combine them with 80% ethanol in a ratio of 1:3.
Steep the chopped root mixture in the ethanol solution for 2 weeks in a cool dark place to allow for infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the tincture.
Filter the tincture through a coffee filter and bottle it in dark glass containers with tight-fitting lids to preserve it.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis tinctures helps with loss of appetite because it contains quinine, a compound known for its ability to stimulate the digestive system and increase appetite. The tincture's alkaloid properties help to improve metabolism and boost energy levels, making food more appealing and reducing the sensation of nausea. Additionally, the tincture's expectorant properties can help to clear congestion, further alleviating feelings of lethargy and improving overall appetite. This makes Cinchona officinalis a potential remedy for those struggling with appetite loss.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: Acts as a bitter compound, stimulating the taste buds and increasing the desire to eat by inducing a sensation of hunger.
Quinovic acid glycosides: Exert a mild bitter taste that helps to stimulate appetite by stimulating the digestive system.
Alkaloids (including cinchonine and cinchonidine): Possess a bitter taste that can increase the desire to eat by stimulating the appetite and digestive system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make tinctures for loss of appetite due to their high concentration of alkaloids, particularly quinine, which has stimulant properties.
Barks: They are used to make tinctures for loss of appetite due to their ability to stimulate digestion and increase appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 pounds of fresh cinchona officinalis bark in the early morning to capture optimal medicinal properties.
Dry the harvested cinchona officinalis bark in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2-3 hours.
Chop the dried cinchona officinalis bark into small pieces and combine it with 80-proof vodka in a ratio of 1:3.
Steep the cinchona officinalis and vodka mixture in a glass container with a lid for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa tinctures helps with loss of appetite because of its ability to stimulate digestion and improve gut health. The bioactive compound curcumin present in the tincture has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing inflammation in the digestive tract. This reduction in inflammation promotes the secretion of digestive enzymes, enhancing nutrient absorption and utilization. As a result, curcuma longa tinctures can help increase appetite and promote a healthy digestive system, leading to improved overall well-being. Its benefits also extend to reducing nausea and indigestion.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps stimulate appetite by regulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, promoting hunger hormones like ghrelin.
Tumerone: This constituent has anti-inflammatory properties that may alleviate symptoms of conditions like irritable bowel syndrome, which can lead to loss of appetite.
Volatile oils (including sesquiterpenes): These oils may have a soothing effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy appetite.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcumin, a bioactive compound that stimulates appetite and digestion.
Roots: Contain various active compounds that help improve appetite and reduce nausea.
Buds: May be used to create a tincture that aids in stimulating appetite and improving digestion due to their bioactive properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh curcuma longa rhizomes in late summer or early fall when they are mature and dry.
Clean and dry the harvested rhizomes completely to prevent any contamination or moisture content.
Chop the dried curcuma longa rhizomes into small pieces using a sharp knife or herb cutter.
Combine the chopped rhizome pieces with 80 proof vodka or ethanol at a 1:5 ratio in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera tinctures helps with loss of appetite because they have adaptogenic properties that help regulate the body's stress response. The herb is believed to stimulate appetite by influencing the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, which controls hunger and satiety hormones. Additionally, Withania somnifera may enhance the production of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, which play a role in regulating appetite and food intake. By promoting a balance in these physiological processes, Withania somnifera tinctures can help alleviate loss of appetite and support overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These steroidal lactones help increase appetite by reducing stress and anxiety, which are common causes of appetite loss.
Alkaloids: Withaferin A, a type of alkaloid present in Withania somnifera, helps stimulate appetite by modulating gut hormones and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
Flavonoids: These polyphenolic compounds, such as withanoside, have been shown to improve appetite by enhancing the gut's ability to absorb nutrients and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary source of Withania somnifera's medicinal properties, including adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory compounds that help stimulate appetite.
Leaves: The leaves of Withania somnifera are used in tinctures to provide a concentrated dose of the plant's adaptogenic properties, which can help boost appetite and metabolism.

Fruits: The fruits of Withania somnifera are used to create tinctures that help stimulate appetite due to their high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that support overall health and digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried withania somnifera root in the morning when the plant is at its highest potency.
Chop the harvested root into small pieces to increase its surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped root with 500 milliliters of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks shaking it occasionally every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax quinquefolius tinctures helps with loss of appetite because they contain compounds that promote digestive health and stimulate appetite. The tincture's active ingredients, such as ginsenosides, have been shown to improve nutrient absorption and increase metabolism. This can lead to a reduction in feelings of hunger and an increase in appetite. Additionally, the adaptogenic properties of Panax quinquefolius help the body adapt to stress, which can contribute to appetite loss. By addressing these underlying issues, Panax quinquefolius tinctures may help restore a healthy appetite.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These compounds help stimulate appetite by regulating the balance of neurotransmitters in the brain, specifically increasing the production of dopamine and serotonin.
Phenolic acids: By exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, phenolic acids help reduce stress and inflammation that can contribute to loss of appetite, promoting a healthier appetite.
Terpenoids (specifically, saponins): These compounds may help stimulate appetite by increasing the release of gastrin, a hormone that regulates appetite and digestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain bioactive compounds that stimulate appetite and improve digestion, making them a primary source for tinctures.

Leaves: The leaves of Panax quinquefolius contain various compounds that may contribute to increased appetite and improved metabolism, although their use in tinctures is less documented compared to roots and rhizomes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature panax quinquefolius roots in the fall after the first frost when they are rich in saponins.
Wash the roots in cold water to remove dirt and debris then pat them dry with a clean cloth.
Chop the roots into small pieces and combine them with 80 proof vodka in a ratio of 1:5.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the container every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum tinctures helps with loss of appetite because they contain bioactive compounds such as piperine, which has been shown to stimulate digestion and increase appetite. The tincture's ability to enhance the absorption of essential nutrients also plays a role in encouraging a healthy appetite. Additionally, its warm, spicy flavor can help stimulate the senses and increase the desire to eat. Overall, Piper nigrum tinctures offer a natural solution to support appetite and overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Piperine: Piperine helps stimulate appetite by enhancing the bioavailability of essential nutrients and improving digestion.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Piper nigrum have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive system and promote appetite.
Terpenes: Terpenes found in Piper nigrum have carminative properties, which help alleviate digestive discomfort and promote appetite by reducing bloating and gas.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used for their piperine content, which stimulates appetite and improves digestion.
Leaves: Used for their aromatic and medicinal properties, which can help increase appetite and alleviate digestive issues.
Fruits: Used for their flavor and medicinal properties, which can help stimulate appetite and improve digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh piper nigrum berries in the morning after the dew has evaporated to ensure optimal quality.
Weigh 100 grams of fresh berries and combine them with 500 milliliters of 95 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for two weeks with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding the solids and reserve the liquid.
Transfer the tincture to small dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to two years.
